title: Actual position value smaller than position limit value
cause: The drive detected that the axis moved beyond the configured negative travel range limit, or the position limit parameters were set incorrectly after homing.
resolution: Verify the homing reference, check parameters S-0-0049 and S-0-0050 (position limit values), move the axis back into the allowed range, and clear the fault with the error reset command.

title: Encoder 2: Encoder signals incorrect
cause: The motor or external encoder cable is damaged, loose, or exposed to electromagnetic interference, producing invalid feedback signals.
resolution: Inspect and reseat the encoder cable, confirm correct shielding and grounding, replace the cable if damaged, and re-run the encoder commissioning.

title: Overcurrent in power section
cause: A short circuit in the motor cable or motor winding, or an incorrectly parameterized current loop, caused the output current to exceed the hardware limit.
resolution: Disconnect the motor and measure winding resistance, check the motor cable for shorts, verify the current controller parameters, and restart the drive after the fault is cleared.

title: Undervoltage in mains
cause: The mains supply voltage dropped below the configured threshold during operation, often due to grid disturbances or an undersized supply.
resolution: Measure the supply voltage, confirm it stays within the rated range, check fuses and contactors, and consider a larger supply or a DC bus capacitor module.

title: ctrlX CORE application could not be started
cause: A required app is missing a dependency, the license is invalid, or the snap failed to install correctly after an update.
resolution: Open Diagnostics in ctrlX OS, review the app logs, reinstall or update the affected app, verify the license activation, and restart the app from the Apps page.

title: Fieldbus master not reaching operational state
cause: An EtherCAT or PROFINET device on the bus is missing, misconfigured, or the network topology does not match the configured one.
resolution: Check the physical bus wiring, compare the scanned topology with the project configuration, power-cycle the affected devices, and set the master back to operational.

title: ctrlX IO bus coupler: terminal not responding
cause: A terminal on the IP20 station lost communication because of a loose contact, a defective terminal, or insufficient bus power.
resolution: Re-seat the terminals, verify the system module power supply, replace the suspected terminal, and restart the IO station to re-scan the bus.

title: Analog input module: measuring range exceeded
cause: The signal connected to an analog input terminal is outside its configured measuring range or a sensor is wired incorrectly.
resolution: Check the sensor wiring and signal level, select the correct measuring range in the module configuration, and confirm the scaling parameters.

title: ctrlX SAFETY: discrepancy error on dual-channel input
cause: The two channels of a safety input changed state at different times beyond the configured discrepancy time, indicating a wiring fault or a failing sensor.
resolution: Inspect both channels of the safety input wiring, verify the discrepancy time setting, test the safety device (e.g. e-stop), and acknowledge the safety fault after correction.

title: ctrlX SAFETY: CRC mismatch in safety configuration
cause: The safety configuration loaded on the SAFEX controller does not match the validated checksum, usually after an incomplete download.
resolution: Re-download the validated safety project, verify the CRC reported matches the documented value, and re-run the safety acceptance test.

title: ctrlX CORE: data layer node unavailable
cause: A client requested a Data Layer node that is not provided because the providing app is stopped or the address path is wrong.
resolution: Confirm the providing app is running, browse the Data Layer tree to verify the correct node path, and restart the app if the node is still missing.

title: PLC runtime: cycle time exceeded
cause: The configured PLC task cycle time is too short for the program workload, causing a watchdog timeout.
resolution: Increase the task cycle time, optimize the program to reduce execution time, or move heavy logic to a slower task, then restart the PLC.
